description: OP issuer key-scoping policy. Enumerates each key in the issuer DID document and the credential types each key is governance-authorised to sign. Informational only; the protocol does not enforce these constraints cryptographically. Verifiers MAY consume this document to detect mis-scoped issuance (e.g. an ObserverDelegationCredential signed by #key-3). See AIP v0.8 §3.4.

keys.notes: Retained in verificationMethod only — verifies historical credentials issued under this key (notably the Cryptorefills merchant attestation). MUST NOT be used to sign any new credential.
Primary org root signing key. Lives only as an offline encrypted keyfile under the custodian's control. Signing is a deliberate manual offline action via the opkey CLI.

Scoped on-server signing key for sub-100ms PolicyEvaluationCredential issuance. Held only on the policy evaluator server under restricted file permissions. Scoping is governance-only; the DID document cannot cryptographically enforce credential-type restrictions. Rotate independently of #key-2 if compromised; compromise invalidates evaluation credentials but does not threaten delegation credentials.
